reviews.review_description: I absolutely love music however this app is a huge disappointment. There was so much potential that could have been done with this app but they really are failing. First off, I cannot find many songs at all. I don't know or understand how to unlock more songs and there is hardly anything in there I want to sing and they charge $15 a month. The appearance of the app in the desert especially is highly annoying. Maybe if you could change things in the app it would be better and even purchasing song packs would be nice!!!

Sorry, but there is no way this is worth $15 a month. Kept it for a few months hoping it would improve, but it hasn't. Searching is awful. Decades only go back to the 1980s it seems. Weird that the environments aren't theaters or clubs with an audience. Sometimes shows songs without any lyrics. This could be cool and a lot of fun, but it really needs a lot of work if you're going to charge money for it.

The song selection alone makes this worth getting. Unless the song was recorded by your high school band in your mom’s basement, it’s probably on here. The visual environments are gorgeous and very psychadelic. The extras, like being able to paint in 3D or play the drums, are a great addition. When I first started painting and realized there was depth to it, I got so distracted I forgot to sing! But by the next song I pulled it together and had an absolute blast. This is a wonderful escape and way to experience music.
Where's the R&B category? That's the main genre I care about. I selected 90's hip hop also, and there wasn't much there.

I’ve been a lifelong music lover, and the Realize Music app has completely elevated how my family and I enjoy music together. We started using it after receiving a pair of Meta VR goggles and a subscription for the holidays, and it’s been nonstop fun ever since!  

The app does an incredible job of blending music, creativity, and interactivity. There’s something for everyone, I’m a longtime Deadhead who loves revisiting Grateful Dead jams, my wife enjoys the showtunes library, and our two teenage daughters are always exploring new artists and genres. No matter who’s using it, there’s always something new to discover.  

What really stands out is the versatility. You can sit back and enjoy immersive visuals while listening, pick up virtual paintbrushes to create art that reacts to the rhythm, or grab the mic and drumsticks to perform along. It’s engaging, easy to use, and perfect for music lovers of all kinds.  

Overall, Realize Music is a must-have for anyone who loves connecting with music in new and creative ways. Highly recommended!

First off, let me say this - I am 81 years old.  I absolutely love this game!  A friend showed it to me and I was hooked from the first time I tried it. It takes me into a different dimension.  It makes me feel joy.  I can play songs that I grew up with and new stuff too.  I lose time in this game and when I’m done I feel BETTER.  This is actually super fun!  I highly recommend this to anyone who loves music even if you think you can’t sing - who cares?!? Just try it and see how you YOU feel too.

I've been having a blast with this singing and drumming VR app on my Quest 3! The combination of singing along tons of tunes and playing the drums is super immersive and a great workout. The graphics and sound quality are top-notch, making you feel like you're really on stage. The variety of songs and play modes keeps things fresh and exciting, and it's a great way to let loose and have some fun. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fun and interactive music experience in VR!
   It’s does have some bugs, but the developers are very actively still working on the game and polishing the experience 👍￼

reviews.review_title: Most fun I’ve had in VR this year
reviews.review_description: This game is going to be the next guitar hero.  They still have some work to do on improving the search but its already very fun.  I ended up just using the jukebox mode and  singing whatever came up and ended up playing til the battery ran out.  felt like id just gone for a run when I was done … could def see this becoming a habit.

reviews.review_title: Wow! Can’t wait to keep singing!
reviews.review_description: Such a beautiful world to sing and explore in and soooo many songs! How the worlds shift and change as you sing is really amazing. Every song I could think of was there and I didn’t even scratch the service. Excited to keep playing, singing and learning the words to all of my fave songs.

This game is awesome! It would 100% help someone if they needed to improve their singing, and it’s great for casuals also. The song selection is decent, and it’s fun and interesting.
 The one downside that I have for this game is that if someone tries to play a song that they don’t know/haven’t heard before it’s difficult to sing along. Otherwise, I 100% would recommend this game to anyone.

developer_response.body: Hi mooksvr!  Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review and share your experience with others!  We are thrilled that you are loving the experience!  Keep singing!

reviews.review_description: Great start for this kind of an app.  There's a decent music selection; the environments you perform in add a touch of variety.  All in all, it's a VR karaoke experience, but elevated.  Would love the samples to be a bit longer to really get a feel for it, but a promising start.  Looking forward to seeing this one get developed further.

developer_response.body:
Hi ddefrehn, 

Thank you for taking the time to write a review of SING!  We hear you about wanting longer samples - unfortunately we are restricted to 30 seconds of the free samples by the labels.  We are playing with a creative way to give a longer sample song, but at this point, this is what we are working with.  We are continuing to work on this game every day - you will see lots of new features and improvements in the next few months!

reviews.review_description: I was blown away by the immersive visuals in Realize Sing and the stars flowing when you sing - I normally don’t sing aloud very much but this reduces my intimidation and it is so much fun. Great songs to choose from.

reviews.review_title: Great Game - Makes me Want to SING!
reviews.review_description: I love this VR experience.  I'm not a singer but the interactive environment, amazing visuals, diverse catalogue and fun interface just make me want to sing out loud (much to the amusement of my family listening in).  I found plenty of old favorite songs and the wide ranging catalogue allows for some new music exploration.  Enjoy!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review!  We love hearing that the whole family is getting involved!  It's awesome you were able to find your old favorites, there will be a huge influx of sings coming in the next few weeks!  Keep on Singing!

reviews.review_title: Oh maaaan,  there’s so much music!
reviews.review_description: I can’t believe anyone would say that the music selections are limited in here.  They must be using it wrong.  There’s SO MUCH to choose from.  You just need to click “catalog” and then you can search for whatever you want.  There’s something for everyone in my family.  I love Kendrick and Biggie my mom loves Billie Eilish, Chappell Roan and Stevie Nicks, but there’s pop, rock, punk, oldies,  and pretty much everything else you can imagine.  I’m HOOOOOOOKED on this game.  Can’t wait to see what’s next! The devs seem to have a lot of plans as mentioned in their comments on other reviews.  100 recommend!

reviews.review_title: Unashamedly a Siren in my own home
reviews.review_description: Belting out tunes in this VR space is mesmerizing, allowing you to take that private concert in the confines of one's shower into the living room.  You can drum and/or paint while singing to whatever song you like in this huge catalogue.  The inclusive settings allow you to use speech to text or typed searches, but there are also presets if indecisive.  I appreciate the effort to have music that expands well beyond the mainstream songs.  There are also instrumental versions, for those who want to make up their own lyrics on the fly.  The environments are stunning as well.  While this game offers a pay per song or subscription option, it have a really neat hover to preview prior to selection, and then when considering the purchasing option, you can try a short version of the song after selection.

developer_response.body:
Hi intjmega!  
Thank you SO MUCH for your review of Sing!  We are thrilled that you are having such a great experience in the game!  Please continue to play - we are working every day to make it even better. We have a community discord where players can suggest features/improvements.  We take user feedback very seriously - some of the best ideas for improvements have come from our community.  Please consider joining the discord community.  https://discord.com/channels/1300793872716267552/1300793872716267555

reviews.review_description: This is a karaoke game! You can actually sing along and it will show lyrics for whatever song you are playing! I'm not a singer, but you can comfortably sing this without anyone watching because I am a bad singer lol. It would be nice if the drum set produces sounds to play along with the music you are singing. For people who don't subscribe, there should be an option for free songs instead of just a bunch of short demo. Free songs can be limited Hopefully as a starter before subscribing. Anyway, highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Hi DeafyPro - thank you so much for your review!  Your feedback on the drum sounds is received and we are playing with the idea of creating that option - you're not the only one to have requested this. Regarding subscription - we have listened to player feedback on this and as a result we are offering individual song purchase for those who wish not to subscribe.  About the short demos - 30 second demo samples are the max length allowed by the labels. We'd make them longer if we could!

reviews.review_description:
I'm one of those people that enjoys singing, but doesn't want to torture everyone around me with it. Realize Sing really lets me belt out some tunes while being immersed in some wild visuals. It feels really good. I am a little embarrassed at how many songs I THOUGHT I knew the lyrics to, but at least the app is helping me learn. 😉 

I'm excited to see what gets added over time.

developer_response.body: Awesome to hear that you are loving the experience!  We all joke about the songs we *thought* we knew by heart, but were singing the wrong lyrics all these years!  Maybe we should do a contest about funniest mis-heard lyrics on our socials!

reviews.review_description: Are you supposed to hear your voice coming through the headset speakers with the music when you sing? I’m very interested in subscribing to this based on what I’ve seen, but if I can’t hear how I really sound with the music, it makes a huge difference. I figured that was why I had to allow the app to have access to to my microphone, but I still can’t hear my voice “within” the instrumentals.

﻿﻿What an astonishing, relaxing, fun and beautiful experience this is. So much music to choose from, radically different immersive environments to choose from in which you can listen to the music, make your world richer by singing along to the scrolling lyrics and/or playing the virtual drums that appear. Stimulating and delightful fun. Recommended A+++

developer_response.body: Hi Woger!  Thank you so much for taking the time to share your impressions of Sing!  We really appreciate your feedback and kind words!  Keep Singing!

reviews.review_description: I had been meh on previous VR games, but this is the first one that really combined fun, creativity, learning, exercise, and (best of all) self-confidence all into one. My children love it, too. You have to try it’so see what I mean, just incredible. Thank you to the designers - my whole family

developer_response.body: Hey Mills!  Thank you for taking the time to share your experience and impressions of Sing!  We are thrilled that you love the game and that you are sharing it with your whole family!  Hope to see you on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_title: Fun(ny) for the whole fam w/ some quirks
reviews.review_description: This is one of those games you’ll be either all-in or all-out. It’s a solo experience but fun(ny) for anyone in the same room.  I tested both the early-access unlocked full-catalog version and the free version, and this review covers both.  The visuals are colorful and immersive, with each song dropping you into a unique 3D world.  Lyrics scroll upward in a Star Wars–style crawl, though they can overlap occasionally (especially on tracks with multiple singers).  Singing effects like shooting stars and fireworks can be toned down in settings if they’re distracting.  The app is free to download, and you can browse the catalog without subscribing. Free players get unlimited replays of 12 short sample songs.  Full access requires a monthly or annual subscription (understandable for licensed music), and the catalog has grown a lot since early testing... even if fellow Swifties may find fewer options.  Search can be slow, but I still found plenty of great pop and ballad choices in my vocal range.  As someone who loves to sing but gets stage-shy, this game makes me feel like a superstar.  There are extra activities like painting and drumming, but the singing is the highlight.  Quirks aside, it’s fun, vibrant, and worth trying... especially if you love to sing or want to gift a subscription.

developer_response.body: Hey Muggito, Thank you so much for taking the time to write this review.  The search feature is a work in progress for sure. It is learning and refining with each interaction from customers. The more people use it, the better it will get.  Thank you again for trying it out!

reviews.review_title: A simple game that knows what it is
reviews.review_description: Quite simply, it's a virtual karaoke machine. It's a great way to sing your heart out in the comfort of your own room, and it is nice that there is a decently sized tracklist for you to try out with different genres. The game is definitely centered around singing, but offers other ways to play, with drums and a paintbrush. Sadly I was quite disappointed with drums, as someone who loves drums, they felt quite weak and had no impact in any of the songs I played. While the latter two other tools are not flushed out as much, they can still provide a good experience if you can immerse youself in it enough. My main issue with the game is the subscription service. The game is clearly meant only for paying users, as free players get 30-second sections of random songs only. I feel like a free player should get access to at least 1 full song, as everyone just deserves to at least try the experience of one song. I would also love another tier of purchase, a flat one-time fee for the game, and maybe you could only get a random selection of a few full songs a week, but a better option for people who do not prefer subscription-based purchases. Overall, it's a simple game that knows what it is, and I think it is a game you can't go wrong with if you enjoy singing.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! We are playing with the idea of adding sound options for the drums.  Thank you for the feedback on that.  Regarding subscriptions - we heard players ' feedback on that and as a result we added the option to purchase individual songs and will be adding song packs (multiple songs) in the very near future.  Thank you on the suggestion about one free song or flat fee for the game - unfortunately, since we are licensing the entire catalogs from the biggest labels in the world, this is not feasible and 30 second samples were the max that the labels would allow us to offer - but we definitely hear you - and will continue to brainstorm on how to make that experience better from the get-go.  In light of this information, would you consider a re-review? Regardless, thank you for taking the time - we appreciate it and we'll continue every day to make improvements to Sing!

reviews.review_description: I was surprised at how GOOD I feel after a 20 min session singing. I found songs I used to love but had forgotten about and so many of my all time favorites.  The catalog is insane.  I have no idea how they managed to get all this music in there.  I read from some of the developer responses to other reviews that they are adding another massive catalog in the next few weeks.  I can't wait to spend hours in here exploring all this music.  This is really special.  Well done!

developer_response.body:
Hey Willis!  
thank you so much for taking the time to play and share your impressions of Sing!  Keep up the singing and hope to see your username on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_description:
Based on the overwhelmingly positive opinion, I must not be the target audience, even though I went into it thinking it was perfect for me. 

If you are looking for the excitement or "interaction" of the singing in Rockband, it won't be found here.

I was given access to the catalog for testing purposes. I can't say that I'm impressed or that I would find the subscription price worth it. I had a bit of fun that very quickly got old. To many mainstays of karaoke are missing from the catalog. I was able to mumble through songs with a decent score. It was very much just a less than stellar experience. I guess I was thinking VR is the perfect opportunity to feel like a pro singer on stage and really get pumped up and I don't. 

The positive: The visuals are nice. I didn't notice any bugs or glitches to speak of. The visuals can be relaxing if that's what you are looking for. Check the demo and see if the song collection and visuals make it worth your while! 

I could see someone VERY into karaoke or just singing to relax possibly really enjoying this game but that isn't me.

reviews.review_title: Super fun game, so many songs!
reviews.review_description: What a blast this thing is! The library even has songs for folks with really weird tastes like mine haha.  The visuals are amazing and the way they react to you are fantastic!  Get in there, it’s too much fun.

developer_response.body: Weird tastes in music make the world go round!  Thank you for taking the time to try Sing and write a review of our game - we've been working hard on this for two years and we are not going to let up!  Now that we are receiving valuable user feedback it will only get better and better.

reviews.review_description:
I guess this is fine. There's not really a whole lot to it that would make me want to keep coming back. You get to sing songs with lyrics on the screen. And the environment around you changes. There's also a couple of VR interactions that you can play with while singing. It's fine. But it feels a bit empty. Especially if you don't subscribe.

I wish there was recording. Maybe something like Smule? Being able to record "open calls" and have friends or random people on the internet join us? Twitch Sings was like this also. It gave you a sense of community and it made it fun to keep coming back to. After trying this once, I don't really have a need to try it again. 

My suggestions:

-Add Avatars
-Add recording performances
-Add multiplayer rooms
-With an avatar, also add the LIV Creator Kit to make fun videos. 
-Let us record open calls and join open calls. 


Make it more of a social experience. If I just want to sing to a song, I can do that without wearing my headset.

At least you can get in there for free and search to find that the song you might want to sing is not in the “over a million songs”. Yes, they have a huge library of songs and there are some very popular songs in there, but a fair bit of them that I have searched for, that I enjoy, are not an option. Then, when you search for them, it’s just going to be a wild list of songs with similar words in the title as you can’t sort it out very well to do something like, “find more songs by this artist”.
I get the concept, it is interesting, but for $10 a month or $2.99 a song, to “vomit” stars out of your mouth to some of the simplest karaoke format I have ever seen, just doesn’t feel worth it. Sure, the environments are cool, you can paint in 3D and bop on some drums too if you want. But, I had a plug-in for my media player one time that did karaoke lyrics the same way. There needs to be something to hook you and the 30 second demo songs don’t do it for me. Though, I did get to do a test build and had access to the full library, I still felt like it was not worth the prices that they are charging. 
It is simple, puts you in your own world, lets you belt out the song the way you want to as it isn’t very strict on scoring, nor does it care if you even sing, so long as you do something you get points. Points add up to XP, which gives you a total score to show up on a leaderboard. I don’t even mind the format in which you are just in a virtual space singing words that float in the distance. But, with that, there does need to be more options, like turning off that “voice” effect or changing it to something else. 
I feel like this is more like a 2.5 star moment as the “free” game is absolutely useless unless you subscribe and really has no point other than to give you generic lyrics, which might not even look like they belong to that song. I know kids would enjoy this as they will be in their own space to sing, but make sure you know what song they are selecting, as the lyrics ar

Sing is a fun and engaging karaoke platform for VR. There is an extensive library of songs from multiple genres to fit everyone's music style. The trippy graphics are immersive, and I find myself just staring around the atmosphere, singing away while belting out a drum solo on the optional drumset. 
You earn points for performance that allow you to level up. I also enjoy that you can hum or even whistle to a song if that is your preference.  
I totally recommend Sing as it's been my goto for when I want a gaming break and feel a song inside me wanting to come out.

developer_response.body:
Hey LatissaKatrice, 

Thank you so much for taking the time to review and recommend Sing!  Your feedback is valuable as we continue to work on this game, improving the core and adding new features.  Please continue to come sing with us and I hope to see your name on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_description: Was debating whether this warrants a 3 or 4 stars. You will either like it or stay away from it I feel. Why? It has some of your hits songs in several categories of music. I feel like you should be able to search for any song and buy it. While there's hit songs its limited. You can download the app and see what all is there before you buy a song or monthly subscription for the songs. Feels like it could rate your karaoke stills a lot better it just feels like a karaoke experience without feedback like a meditation karaoke experience is what I would call it. you can pick up the musical objects all look around while singing. I didn't experience any bugs or glitches so it was a smooth experience just left a lot of potential out there in updates or competition.

developer_response.body:
Hey Brannan, 
Thank you for taking the time to review Sing.  We are constantly working to improve the game - including developing more robust feedback elements as you mentioned.  We have licensed 3 of the largest catalogs of music in the world (Universal, Warner and Sony) and music will continue to populate in the game as we unlock additional publishing catalogs and larger indie label content.  This game is a live operation - in no way are we done working on it - as long as it exists we will continue to improve it and add features.  Feedback like yours is key to making the best possible music experience, so thank you again for your review!!  Join our discord to share more feedback and learn about feature updates: https://discord.gg/r8mTAbpEFs

reviews.review_title: Best use of music in a game!!!
reviews.review_description: I can’t believe how MUCH good music there is in here.  Can’t wait to dig even deeper.  I didn’t know how much I loved to sing!   Highly recommend!!!

reviews.review_title: In VR No One Can Hear You Sing!
reviews.review_description: Realize Sing is surprisingly fun for anyone who loves to sing. It is essentially a solo karaoke game that has a good library of titles you can sing along to. There are some classic bangers in it, but with licensing issues you are not going to find every song you want. In the game, you sing along to a scrolling Star Wars style slow crawl of lyrics in a 360-degree 3D world. While you are in it, you also get a set of virtual drums to play, as well as a 3D pen to paint your surroundings. It’s great if you are not in the mood for singing, and it’s also a cool place to hang out and listen to your tracks while browsing the internet in a pop out window. My only complaint is the subscription model but for what you get it's a great deal.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to write a review.  We are thrilled that you are enjoying the game!  One thing to share with you, that might not have been obvious - for those players who do not like subscriptions we added the ability to purchase individual tracks (you can purchase ANY of the tracks in our entire catalog of over 1 Million songs)  It was because of user feedback that we decided to offer that option! If this changes your impression please consider a re-review!  Please continue to come back - we have a bunch of enhancements and features that we'll be deploying in the coming weeks and a TON more music is being added to the catalog!

reviews.review_description:
The amount of songs is very limited and the search option doesn't do a thing. You can search the band Nirvana and like 20 things come up that aren't even remotely related to Nirvana. So what is the point of having a search? You can click '80s or pop or rock and the search shows the same thing every time. Pointless.

You also can't record yourself I'm assuming because of copyrights on the song. So that's pretty lame. Wish I hadn't bothered to sign up. I'm out $10.87 because I won't use this. LAME!

id say its worth it if the subscription was about half price
i only bought one song because $3 is kinda pushing it 
$1.99 would be better js overall pretty fun game if you got the cash for the songs or sub

developer_response.body:
Hey Donny, 
thank you for your review!  We are working to make the songs as affordable as possible.  One new feature that is coming soon is song packs - these will reduce the cost of individual tracks by bundling them together.  Other than that - the subscription will always be the best value as it unlocks the entire catalog of well over 1 Million songs.  We also have a huge catalog being added to the game as I type this and it should be demonstrable by Dec 1.

reviews.review_description: Really enjoyed this - it’s care free and fun, easy to get started and kinda encouraging to carry on. The shower, my car, and now with the headset on - looking forward to singing more.

developer_response.body:
Hi Marrilee, 
Thank you so much for taking the time out of your day to share your feedback of Sing!  Hope to see your name on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_description: This app is so much fun - everyone needs to try this. I'm a transformed shower singer to a VR singer. Absolutely love this app and they have every song imaginable.

developer_response.body:
Hey Gamer, 
Thanks you so much for taking the time to share your experience!  Keep singing!

reviews.review_title: Fun if you like solo karaoke
reviews.review_description: Lots of songs in the library, but only a couple that I want to sing alone that are in my vocal range. Heavy on AI art, and while drumming and painting are fun they don't really impact anything.

In my opinion, this shouldn't have released yet, or it should've been in early access.  Since the dev does seem to be active and making regular changes, I'm going to be generous and review it as though it were an E.A. release. (Especially considering they have a roadmap for updates.)

I see the potential, and I understand the vision for this, but I don't think it lives up to that vision.  It feels stuck trying to be a "wellness" app and a serious competitive karaoke game, but it succeeds at neither.  It gives me specific scores and yet no feedback on why, and I'm left confused as to why I sang a song perfectly well and got 95%. I gain levels, but I see no use for them other than watching numbers go up (which doesn't appeal to me).  It's branded as not karaoke, and yet it literally has karaoke songs (also ironically saw at least some with no lyrics).  I tried searching songs I would want to sing as well as ones I know others would want to sing, and I was left severely wanting (and quite frustrated at the horrible searching function).

I get that the game has licensing requirements, but I also don't really see myself ever feeling like it's worth the cost for the experience.  Possibly in the future it can live up to that, but I'm not sure how.  I personally would never do more than spending maybe $25-50 (cost of most full games) for a permanent unlock of all the songs I'd personally want, and I might regret it later.

I would rate this 2-stars, but because the dev is active and I see the potential with it, I'll be generous and give it 3-stars.  It's a pretty meh experience, even having tested it with all songs unlocked.  I think if it had the promised playlist feature, favoriting songs, better catalog, all the bugs fixed, etc. I could probably bump this up to a 4-star in the future and if the pricing were better, or at least had a reasonable starter bundle of songs you pick for a fair price, it could be a 5-star.

I have high hopes for this.  Will update as I'm able.

First off, I am not one to do Karaoke at a club, I won't even do it in social VR apps that have karaoke rooms (that do not have a fee).  That being said, this is perfect for people like myself who just want to belt out songs like we do in the shower or in the car when nobody is listening!  I do appreciate the developers constantly working on the app and I realize it is a work in progress.  The possibilities are there for this to be a total hit once more recognizable songs are added and some fixes are made.  It is nice to see devs who stand behind their product and work on it to make the consumer happy.  Personally, I would probably not be likely to subscribe, but may purchase by album if that choice is added in the future.  For instance, I'm a huge Twenty One Pilots fan so I immediately searched for their songs and I believe I found 3, but if there were more and the option to purchase by album it would be even better.  Which brings me to my other con, most of the songs are "B side" songs, they are by popular, mainstream, artists, but they are not their recognizable songs.  I have a variety of music genres I like, from 70s/80s rock all the way to some more modern Alternative bands.  Alot of the searches I did resulted in nothing by the particular band.  Some examples are ELO, Lorde, Pink Floyd, and Toto to name a few.  I also did not particularly care for the search system "resetting" itself after a search, which I think I read that is being worked on.  I did see someone mention the "background" blocking some of the lyrics and I did notice that happening, but it was songs I know the words to so it was not an issue for me.  I believe one of the songs it happened with was "Sweet Home Alabama" and possibly "Like A Virgin" by Madonna.  I do love the backgrounds and the ability to change them, I would like to favorite certain backgrounds as well as favorite songs (which may be available, I need to check back into that.  Again, there is alot of potential!! (Multiplayer???)

developer_response.body:
Hi Gigi!  Thank you for taking the time to share your experience of Sing!  You are so correct - we are constantly working on making the experience better - taking feedback from our dear players and incorporating new creative ideas to improver the player's experience.  


We are adding new music and developing ways for the most recognizable songs to automatically percolate to the top of the recommendations.  As we add agreements with additional music publishers, our catalog of well known songs will only grow and grow!  

We will be adding the option to purchase song bundles like you mentioned!  

I thought it was strange that your search for Twenty one Pilots only returned 3 songs so I searched on the backend.  We have more than 40 songs from them in the system - some of them are rarities and live tracks but more than 30 are standard album cuts including "Stressed Out" - their biggest hit.  Because of your feedback note, we are investigating why the search feature is not delivering the results it should.  This is our biggest issue/challenge at the moment and please know that we are working hard to resolve this. 

Some of the artists you searched for are not in the catalog yet due to restrictions about those specific artists.  Pink Floyd, ELO, Toto, etc.  These artists require additional permissions in the licensing to be included but we are working on unlocking those artists (and others too)

Great note about wanting to *favorite* backgrounds and songs - adding that to our task roadmap.  Thank you again for the great notes!  We do care very much about the feedback from our players.  Please check back in and please also consider leaving a re-review. :)

reviews.review_title: great potential but demo  is too short
reviews.review_description: i love what i think this game is trying to do but i couldn't really experience it's because they only give you a 30 second clip of one song. also the drums in the game we're not making any sounds for me.

developer_response.body:
Hey Diomark!  
Thank you for your review.  We wanted to give players a taste of what they can expect inside Sing so we lobbied the labels to be able to demonstrate the experience by allowing 30 second sample experiences of 12 different songs.  You should be able to try 12 different songs across multiple genres, giving you a taste of the different environments and features of the game - please have another look and let us know if you are able to access all 12 song samples.  You can purchase/unlock individual songs  *or* for the best value you can unlock the entire catalog with a monthly or yearly subscription.  Feedback like yours is valuable as we continue to work on this game every day, adding features and improvements. We have a discord, please consider joining for feedback and updates.  https://discord.gg/r8mTAbpEFs  Thanks again!

reviews.review_description:
I'll start with the positive. I had a blast playing this! I always loved singing, but until Rock Band (the game), I was always too embarrassed to sing except very quietly to myself. Rock Band didn't teach me to sing. It taught me to sing anyway. This game let's me sing and if you decide to try it, sing as if you are the best singer in the world, even if you have to go do it by yourself. Unfortunately, I can't give the game full credit for making singing fun. It gets in the way as much as it helps.

It *does* have a lot of music, but I don't know most of it. It has artists I know and love, but even a lot of those are songs I didn't know. There are some great songs in there but they aren't easy to find. It's easiest if you search by artist name. I did find some great songs I know, but it was not easy, and once you find and play a song, it resets your search. The song list must be auto generated because there are duplicates in there.

The graphics are clean and look good. The backgrounds are generally fantasy art that reminds me of Roger Dean. I was playing seated unfortunately some of the UI was obscured by the virtual platform I was standing on. They display an info card when a song starts, which is good, but if the song starts the singing early, the words are obscured. I had the same problem with the song title card at the end. I also had one case where the "background" obscure the lyrics. Sometime lyrics will obscure lyrics. 

And speaking of lyrics, they get the lyrics from Music Match, but they were wrong or mistimed in many cases. I recommend sticking with songs you know so the lyrics will serve more as a reminder. You don't get any kind of pitch cues so that's more reason to stick with songs you know.

But in the end, if you enjoy singing, love music, and are willing to live with flaws, it's a great time! They do have an extensive future roadmap, so hopefully improvements are on the way.

I received a free copy of this app for play testing.

developer_response.body:
Hi tpancoast, 
Thank you so much for taking the time to write a review and offer your feedback!  We are a small developer and are working every day on this game (and have been for 2 years)!  Your feedback is valuable and touches on a few things that we are working on currently.  

1. Music Catalog  - it IS massive and  searching that catalog is the biggest point of friction for us - we see from user feedback that it can be frustrating to get as many as 10 returned options for the same song.  This is the "problem" with having so much music (blanket licenses) The GOOD NEWS is that the search feature is learning user preferences with each interaction and will continue to improve and evolve as more players use it.   
2. Musixmatch lyrics - MM is a great partner and when we report a mis-timed, mis-synched, or incorrect lyric to them, they usually have it corrected and uploaded within 48 hours.  To this end - we are working on a fix where players can help speed up this process by reporting any lyric issues from within the game.  Much like the search feature - the more players stress test the game, the better and better it will get.  
3. Pitch cues - Yes! You are on it!  We are also working on pitch feedback in real time so that you can measure yourself up to the original song.  We are also working on a vocal range test that will tell you your range and recommend songs within that range for you!  
4. Obscured lyrics - we are aware of this and working on a fix! This only happens in certain songs - so we are trying to identify the common link between those songs so that we can solve this issue.  thank you for sharing this experience.  

Again - thank you so much for your feedback - it means a lot to us that you took the time to review.  Please check back in as we continue to improve the experience and please consider re-reviewing when you do.  Hope to see you on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_title: Just You a Mic and the Music
reviews.review_description: Well, there is also various scenes. But you get to sing to various songs. Some of the songs I found in their library surprised me. It's a lot of fun for a shower songbird such as myself. The interface makes it pretty easy to find songs. It's a pretty decent little app.

developer_response.body: We're glad you've enjoyed our game! Thank you so much for the review!

reviews.review_description: literally every single song you need to pay for free samples last for the 30 seconds please add an actual free song
So fun! We should all be singing! Good for you too, this is my new wellness ritual! 🫶🏽

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your review!  We are thrilled that you are enjoying the game!  Please join our discord to share feedback and learn about new features! https://discord.gg/r8mTAbpEFs

reviews.review_title: Alot of fun, potential to be great
reviews.review_description: I stumbled upon this and then sang more this evening than I have in the past 25 year . This is a novel use of VR and I thought it was pretty great overall. There's both a subscription model and an ability to purchase individual songs. If you use this on a regular basis I think the subscription is a really good deal.  There's a lot of science on how beneficial singing is for you and I think this is a great app to help make this into a habit. In my experience the search is a bit inconsistent. I'm not sure if that's due to my internet connection or something on their side. Regardless, once you find a few groups that you are into it's a lot of fun. For me, I really enjoyed the songs from "The Police". I don't think there's a favorite feature yet, I think they are working on a companion mobile phone app and there's that feature there. Overall, this is a great idea IMO, and with some time I think this app will be really good.

It's inherently fun to sing along with your fave songs and coupled with the gorgeous, immersive visuals this is a great experience that allows you to really lose yourself in the music. Impressively broad and deep music catalog to choose from with old school jams and current hits. Great for those who may be too embarrassed to do karoake in public and also good for group/party fun. STRONG offering to the music space for those who aren't looking for the typical beat matching/music rhythm gameplay format. I see lots of potential for cool collabs w/ artists .

developer_response.body:
Thank you Jeremy!  

We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your experience of Sing!  We are working hard every day to keep improving the experience and make that deep catalog even deeper!  Love your note about cool collabs with artists too!  We are dreaming the same dream!  Thank you again!   Hope you keep singing  - we'll be looking for you on the leaderboards!

reviews.review_title: How did I live without this?!?
reviews.review_description: I am obsessed with this game.  I can’t wait for some new environments to be released.  This has totally captured my imagination and attention.  And dang it if I do t feel better each time I play!  It really does have an uplifting effect!!
I love this game. It is a must try for people who love to enjoy all types of music and sing their hearts out.

This game has potential and kept me entertained for a while. I enjoy singing but not so much in a crowd. That's what's nice about Sing, it's a solo act. I would love to see more in the future but cost may be an issue for myself and others. I had fun playing the drums and even painting while singing was a hoot. The visuals are appealing to the eye. All in all, I had fun would recommend it to my friends who love to sing.

developer_response.body: Hi Karma!  Thanks so much for taking the time to review Sing!  We are working hard to add more music and features to the game and also to provide bundles which will reduce the per-song cost for those who prefer the a la carte version.  Thank you for your positive comments about the drumming and painting features too!  It sounds like you had a good time playing!  - please consider a re-review.

reviews.review_description: Like a few others have mentioned, this should be Early Access title or have it clearly labeled that it's still in Beta.  I'm surprised that it's already available as a 'full' release.  The karaoke/lyrics system is wonky and unreliable.  In some songs, you get the word-by word guide, and in some nothing at all.  Also the shimmering and disappearing lyrics is distracting and don't see a way to change that.  Sometimes the toasts or pop-up labels with the song info come up in front of the lyrics while the songs are still going on.  The search system  and browsing through the catalog is clunky as well (It could use more categories, and better categorization).  I'm sure these are things that can be fixed, but for an app that has "Sing" in it's title it needs of lot minor quirks ironed out.  Once you do get into a song, however, it is fun, and the drums are great to use either while singing and especially during instrumental intervals.  I didn't use much of the paintbrush as it didn't add anything to the experience for me, but maybe for others it will.

Sing shows good potential for a virtual karaoke experience. The addition of 360 degree environments, drum pads to beat on, and a 3d art tool offers decent distraction making things more engaging. There is also quite a large collection of music available through the subscription or individual purchase. However, there is no separate volume control for the vocals on songs, and you don't hear your own voice play through the speakers. This means you are just singing along to the normal version of the track. It is harder to justify paying for music through here when this is the case. The score tracking seems to only listen for how much you are singing, not how well, so you do not get feedback on your performance. Additionally, many song's lyrics are out of sync or missing entirely - making them almost worthless here.

developer_response.body:
Hi Dirk, 
Thank you so much for taking the time to try Sing and post a review.  I am excited to tell you that we are launching (in December) the ability to fade down the original track vocals (just as you described).  We've been working on this feature for many months but couldn't get it over the line in time for our launch - we've got it working now and it's going to be awesome - just need to smooth out a few things regarding the UI changes to add this in. What do you think we should call that feature?  

The other thing that we are adding is player's vocal pitch detection, so you'll be able to see how close you are to the original singer's pitch and have optional score tracking around that too.  

Regarding the missing lyrics/sync issues on some tracks - we get our lyrics served by a company called MusixMatch - they are really responsive about re-syncing and adding missing lyrics - it usually takes less than 48 hours once we put in a request.  We need to have an easy way for players to identify when a song needs lyric attention.  Thank you for bringing this up - this is a feature that I will add to the development roadmap as well.  I hope you'll come back and give Sing another try and consider a re-review considering these new features.  Thanks again for the review!

reviews.review_title: Immersive, but needs some UI Improvement
reviews.review_description:
This VR karaoke game offers a surprisingly immersive singing experience. The combination of music, visuals, and atmosphere gives it a calming, almost meditative feel, and the song selection is genuinely great. I also appreciate that you can browse the full catalog before deciding to subscribe.

That said, a few issues keep it from a higher rating. The search function isn’t always accurate and sometimes fails to load, so you may end up scrolling to find what you want. The lyrics also don’t fully fade out before new lines appear, creating a distracting layered effect that makes them harder to read while singing.

Overall, it’s a nice game with a strong foundation and a large catalog, but it could really shine with some fixes to the search system and interface.

developer_response.body: Hey AngelFire, Thank you so much for taking the time to write this review.  The search feature is a work in progress for sure. It is learning and refining with each interaction from customers. The more people use it, the better it will get. We will take your feedback about the lyrics to heart and look for solutions to improve their presentation.  We've been working hard on this for 2 years. We will keep improving it as we go!  We hope you'll come back and try it again and consider a re-review.  Thank you again for trying it out!
